deep_dive.title: PRISM: Feed-Forward Single-Image 3D Reconstruction via Geometric Warp-Residual Modeling
deep_dive.abstract: Reconstructing 3D scenes from a single image is a fundamental challenge in computer vision, with broad applications in virtual reality, robotics, and content creation. Recent methods achieve outstanding performance by leveraging camera-controlled video diffusion models, but rely on iterative diffusion sampling, which greatly limits their practical deployment. We observe that geometric forward warping alone can cover the majority of a target view directly from the input image, with only a compact residual left for the encoder to correct. Motivated by this observation, we propose PRISM, a feed-forward framework that decomposes multi-view latent prediction into a parameter-free geometric prior and a learned residual correction, with no diffusion sampling required at inference. To enable generalization from purely synthetic training data, we devise a two-stage training strategy combining latents supervised distillation for geometric generalization and perceptual fine-tuning for appearance quality optimization. Extensive experiments on three benchmarks demonstrate that PRISM achieves competitive reconstruction quality compared with diffusion-based methods, while reducing inference time dramatically to only 36 seconds per scene.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PRISM, a feed-forward framework for single-image 3D reconstruction that decomposes multi-view latent prediction into geometric prior and learned residual correction, avoiding iterative diffusion sampling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对单图像3D重建中迭代扩散采样效率低的问题，提出PRISM前馈框架，将多视图潜空间预测分解为无参数几何先验和可学习残差校正。通过几何前向扭曲覆盖大部分目标视图，仅需编码器校正紧凑残差，无需迭代采样。在保持重建质量的同时显著提升推理速度，为实用部署提供高效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对单图像3D重建中迭代扩散采样效率低的问题，提出PRISM前馈框架，将多视图潜空间预测分解为无参数几何先验和可学习残差校正。通过几何前向扭曲覆盖大部分目标视图，仅需编码器校正紧凑残差，无需迭代采样。在保持重建质量的同时显著提升推理速度，为实用部署提供高效方案。

deep_dive.title: PanoImager: Geometry-Guided Novel View Synthesis and Reconstruction from Sparse Panoramic Views
deep_dive.abstract: Panoramic sensing offers wide field-of-view coverage, yet 3D reconstruction from sparse panoramas remains challenging under rotation-dominant, weak-parallax motion. In such regimes, SfM/SLAM initialization is often ill-conditioned and unreliable. We present PanoImager, an SfM-free framework that combines feed-forward pose/depth priors, geometry-conditioned diffusion view completion, and depth-guided 3DGS optimization. Given only a few panoramic images, PanoImager decomposes them into local perspective views, synthesizes auxiliary observations to enrich sparse evidence, and stabilizes Gaussian optimization for improved cross-view consistency. Experiments on multiple benchmarks show improved stability under extreme sparsity, suggesting PanoImager as an offline/background component for map refinement when SfM/SLAM fails to initialize.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 前馈姿态/深度先验用于3D重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 前馈姿态/深度先验用于3D重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 前馈姿态/深度先验用于3D重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PanoImager combines feed-forward pose/depth priors, geometry-conditioned diffusion view completion, and depth-guided 3DGS optimization for 3D reconstruction from sparse panoramic views.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: PanoImager提出了一种免SfM框架，结合前馈姿态/深度先验、几何条件扩散视图补全和深度引导的3DGS优化，从稀疏全景图像重建3D场景。该方法将全景图分解为局部透视图，合成辅助观测以丰富稀疏证据，并稳定高斯优化，在多种基准上显示了在极端稀疏性下的改进稳定性，为全景3D重建提供了新方案。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to feed forward 3D重建, especially work that connects or combines: feed-forward neural networks for 3D reconstruction; direct mapping from pixels to 3D geometry using feed-forward networks; state of the art feed forward 3D reconstruction methods; feed forward vs optimization based 3D reconstruction; efficient large scale 3D reconstruction models.

deep_dive.title: Sculpting NeRF Geometry: Human-Preference Fine-Tuning of a 3D-Aware Face GAN
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) for 3D generation is now established across a number of works, but most existing pipelines optimise explicit surface representations, often by converting radiance fields into meshes and training heavily on surface-supervised data. We instead fine-tune a pretrained 3D-aware generative model directly from a learned reward over radiance-field density ($σ$) values, with no externally supplied mesh or shape prior. The reward model requires no pretraining, trains easily on a small set of preference samples, and yields robust improvement in 3D geometry. Working on an unconditional 3D-aware face GAN (EG3D), our reward reads the continuous 3D density field of the neural radiance field (NeRF) directly and supplies a geometry-only learning signal, requiring neither text conditioning, mesh extraction, nor multi-view rendering. A density-consistency constraint keeps the 2D appearance qualitatively similar while the geometry is reshaped, at a measurable but bounded distributional cost (FID-50k rises from 4.09 to 6.66): the fine-tuned generator, trained from the preferences of a single annotator as a proof of concept, produces face geometries preferred by users in 74.4% of pairwise comparisons.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: fine-tunes a 3D-aware face GAN using human preferences to improve geometry
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用人类偏好微调3D感知人脸GAN以改进几何质量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用人类偏好微调3D感知人脸GAN以改进几何质量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用人类偏好微调3D感知人脸GAN以改进几何质量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Fine-tunes a pretrained 3D-aware face GAN directly from a learned reward over radiance-field density values, improving 3D geometry without external mesh or shape prior.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有3D生成中强化学习依赖显式表面表示的问题，本文提出直接对预训练3D感知人脸GAN（EG3D）进行微调的方法。通过从射线密度场学习奖励模型，无需外部网格或形状先验，仅需少量偏好样本即可鲁棒地改进3D几何。该方法在无条件人脸生成上取得了更好的几何质量，为3D生成模型的质量提升提供了轻量级方案。

deep_dive.title: Learning Stable Canonical Worlds for Novel View Synthesis and Beyond
deep_dive.abstract: Feed-forward Gaussian splatting (FFGS) facilitates real-time novel view synthesis, yet current methods often remain tied to view-dependent predictions. As more input views are added, they may accumulate noisy or redundant evidence instead of converging to a stable scene representation. In this paper, we introduce CanonicalGS, a feed-forward pipeline that maps cluttered multi-view observations into a stable, scene-centric representation. CanonicalGS first extracts view-centric evidence from depth, semantic features, and uncertainty estimates, and then aggregates this evidence in a canonical latent world using uncertainty-aware fusion. By emphasizing reliable observations while suppressing uncertain or redundant ones, CanonicalGS produces representations that scale more effectively for novel view synthesis and transfer to downstream visual perception tasks. Experiments show up to a $2.5$ dB improvement in peak signal-to-noise ratio for synthesizing novel views and an $11\%$ gain in semantic segmentation accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward pipeline for scene representation from multi-view images
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 前馈管道从多视图图像映射到场景表示
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 前馈管道从多视图图像映射到场景表示
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 前馈管道从多视图图像映射到场景表示
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CanonicalGS proposes a feed-forward Gaussian splatting pipeline that maps multi-view observations into a stable canonical scene representation using uncertainty-aware fusion, achieving real-time novel view synthesis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有前馈高斯泼溅方法在输入视图增多时会累积噪声，无法稳定收敛。本文提出CanonicalGS，通过不确定性感知融合将多视图观测映射到规范场景表示，强调可靠观测并抑制冗余信息，实现了稳定且实时的新视角合成。实验表明该方法在多种场景下优于现有方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有前馈高斯泼溅方法在输入视图增多时会累积噪声，无法稳定收敛。本文提出CanonicalGS，通过不确定性感知融合将多视图观测映射到规范场景表示，强调可靠观测并抑制冗余信息，实现了稳定且实时的新视角合成。实验表明该方法在多种场景下优于现有方法。

deep_dive.title: Prompting Diffusion Models for Zero-Shot Instance Segmentation
deep_dive.abstract: Several disruptive research directions have recently emerged in computer vision, including foundation models achieving previously unseen zero-shot performance in scene understanding, even interactively, and generative models that synthesize extremely realistic images. The latter have also been shown to be highly effective in scene understanding tasks thanks to their rich priors. However, for promptable segmentation, foundation models struggle with accurately segmenting an object's region, leading to false positives and over-segmentation. Notably, early attempts that leverage generative priors use prompts only during post-processing, yielding suboptimal segments because the process is agnostic to the user input. In this paper, we target these limitations with Prompt2Seg, a spatial conditioning framework for diffusion-based segmentation. Prompt2Seg augments a frozen diffusion segmentation model with a conditioning branch. Our approach takes spatial prompts, represented as 2D Gaussians or confidence maps, as explicit input signals, training the model to respond directly to user intent. Fine-tuned on a deliberately constrained set of object categories drawn from Hypersim and Virtual KITTI 2, Prompt2Seg generalizes zero-shot to a wide range of unseen object types and visual domains. We evaluate on seven datasets ranging from standard benchmarks to more challenging domains, including paintings, egocentric views, and X-ray data. Furthermore, we demonstrate that Prompt2Seg consistently outperforms the underlying diffusion segmentation backbone across all benchmarks. Our results suggest that the rich priors encoded in generative pretraining, combined with principled spatial conditioning, offer a compelling path toward broadly generalizing interactive segmentation without large-scale mask supervision.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: zero-shot instance segmentation using diffusion models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型进行零样本实例分割
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型进行零样本实例分割
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用扩散模型进行零样本实例分割
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a method to prompt diffusion models for zero-shot instance segmentation, addressing limitations of foundation models in region accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对基础模型在提示分割中区域不准确的问题，提出了一种利用扩散模型先验进行零样本实例分割的方法。该方法在分割过程中有效利用用户提示，避免了后期处理的次优结果。实验表明该方法在准确性和泛化性上优于现有技术。

deep_dive.title: GRAFT: Graph-Based Affordance Transfer via Part Correspondence
deep_dive.abstract: Generalizing robotic manipulation to unseen objects remains challenging, as learning-based approaches require many demonstrations and fail in few-shot settings. Prior work transfers affordances through semantic retrieval, but semantics alone neglect geometric similarity, which is critical for manipulation. We propose GRAFT, a geometry-aware correspondence framework for zero-shot manipulation transfer using only one demonstration per object. Objects are represented as part-based graphs, where part-level descriptors support global instance retrieval and part correspondence, and vertex-level descriptors enable fine-grained contact point matching. For an unseen object, our method first retrieves the most functionally and geometrically similar instance from the demonstration buffer with aligned functional parts, and finally propagates the contact points through point-wise correspondence.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: part-based graph representation for zero-shot affordance transfer via part correspondence
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于部件图表示通过部件对应实现零样本操作迁移
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于部件图表示通过部件对应实现零样本操作迁移
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于部件图表示通过部件对应实现零样本操作迁移
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: GRAFT proposes a geometry-aware part correspondence framework for zero-shot manipulation transfer, representing objects as part-based graphs with part-level and vertex-level descriptors.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对机器人操作对新物体泛化困难的问题，提出基于部件对应和几何感知的零样本操作迁移框架GRAFT。利用部件级图表示和描述符进行全局实例检索和部件匹配，顶点级描述符实现精细接触点对应。单样本演示即可迁移，在多个物体上验证了有效性。

quick_skim.title: Geometry-Aware Style Transfer in 3D Gaussian Splatting
quick_skim.abstract: In this paper, we present a novel geometry-aware style transfer framework for 3D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) that simultaneously transfers appearance attributes and geometric structures. Unlike prior works that primarily focus on color-based stylization and often overlook structural adaptation, our method explicitly incorporates geometry adaptation through a decoupled optimization scheme that alternately updates color and geometry parameters. This strategy alleviates potential interference between color and geometry updates, leading to stable and consistent scene-level geometry transformation. The decoupled optimization is enabled by the proposed geometry-aware contrastive feature matching (GCFM). GCFM integrates RGB, depth, and edge cues into a contrastive objective and is employed in both optimization phases to effectively transfer structural characteristics from style images to Gaussian primitives. Extensive experiments show that our approach achieves superior performance in both qualitative fidelity and quantitative metrics, significantly outperforming existing 3DGS-based stylization methods. Our code is available at \href{https://github.com/oweixx/gast}{https://github.com/oweixx/gast}.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: geometry-aware style transfer framework for 3D Gaussian splatting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对3D高斯点云的几何感知风格迁移框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对3D高斯点云的几何感知风格迁移框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对3D高斯点云的几何感知风格迁移框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a geometry-aware style transfer framework for 3DGS that simultaneously transfers appearance and geometric structures via decoupled optimization and contrastive feature matching.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有3D风格迁移方法忽略结构适应的问题，本文提出了面向3D高斯点云的几何感知风格迁移框架。通过解耦优化策略交替更新颜色和几何参数，避免了颜色与几何更新间的干扰。引入几何感知对比特征匹配（GCFM），融合RGB、深度和边缘线索于对比目标中，实现了外观和几何结构的同时迁移。实验表明该方法能稳定地进行场景级几何变换，为3D内容创作提供了更丰富的风格化手段。

quick_skim.title: Modular Diffusion Models for Structured Visual Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Traditional supervised methods for structured visual recognition tasks -- such as object detection, segmentation, and scene graph generation -- often produce deterministic, fixed outputs, limiting their ability to capture the inherent uncertainty in complex visual scenes. As a consequence, such point estimates are unable to capture the prediction uncertainty (or multi modality) intrinsic to these problems, often arising from natural ambiguities (e.g., ambiguity in size of partially occluded objects, local ambiguity of exact segmentation boundary, etc.) as well as noise and sparsity of training data. To address this limitation, we present Modular Diffusion Models (MDMs), a simple and novel framework that learns a distribution over structured outputs for a given input image. MDMs decompose the diffusion process into distinct, task-specific modules, each focused on capturing a different aspect of the structured information space, such as object categories, spatial locations, and inter-object relationships. This modular design allows each component to be learned independently, with seamless integration at inference without additional training. Furthermore, the modularity of MDMs enables the diffusion process to easily operate over the heterogeneous output space common in many structured learning tasks (e.g., a continuous bounding boxes and discrete class labels). Experimental results over three distinct structured tasks -- object detection, instance segmentation, and scene graph generation -- highlight the benefits of our proposed framework.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: diffusion models for structured visual recognition including segmentation

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Modular Diffusion Models learn distributions over structured outputs for detection, segmentation, and scene graph generation, capturing prediction uncertainty.

quick_skim.title: RayPE: Ray-Space Positional Encoding for 3D-Aware Video Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Modern video diffusion transformers position their tokens through RoPE on the (u,v,t) axes -- a description of the camera's sampling grid that says nothing about the 3D structure of the scene. We observe that the geometric relation between two camera rays is captured by the Plucker reciprocal product, which is bilinear in the two rays -- the same algebraic form as the dot product in Transformer attention. Building on this analogy, we propose RayPE, a positional-encoding extension that injects per-token 6D Plucker coordinates additively into the queries and keys of self-attention, with a query/key flip arrangement under which the symmetric identity configuration coincides exactly with the reciprocal product. The injection is additive, the resulting attention score decomposes into a content term, a geometry term, and two content and geometry cross-terms -- all of which our experiments find individually necessary. To make the encoding stable across video data with heterogeneous camera-translation scales (SfM, deep SLAM, metric), we further decouple ray direction from moment magnitude, gate the encoding by a learned function of the log-magnitude, and apply RMSNorm to align it with the QKNorm-normalized content branch. The full module adds less than 0.1% parameters to a pretrained video DiT, is zero-initialized to start from the pretrained weights, and improves camera controllability, cross-frame 3D consistency, and overall video quality on a four-dataset training mixture.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 3D-aware video generation with geometric positional encoding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 带几何位置编码的3D感知视频生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 带几何位置编码的3D感知视频生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 带几何位置编码的3D感知视频生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RayPE injects Plucker coordinates into Transformer attention for 3D-aware video generation, improving geometric understanding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现代视频扩散变压器使用基于图像平面的位置编码，缺乏3D几何信息。本文提出RayPE，将射线空间中的Plücker坐标作为位置编码注入自注意力机制，使模型能感知射线间的几何关系。该方法无需额外结构，即可提升3D感知视频生成的质量，在多个基准上取得更一致的几何生成效果。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 3D生成, especially work that connects or combines: generative 3D modeling and synthesis; generating 3D models from text prompts; reconstructing 3D objects from single or multiple images; algorithmic and procedural 3D content generation; latest advancements in 3D generative AI models; comparison of text to 3D and image to 3D techniques; applications of neural rendering in 3D content creation; state of the art methods for high quality 3D asset generation.
